wiki.extract: Sonic Corporation, founded as Sonic Drive-In and more commonly known as Sonic, is an American drive-in fast-food chain owned by Inspire Brands, the parent company of Arby's, Jimmy John's, Buffalo Wild Wings, Baskin-Robbins, and Dunkin'. Sonic, founded by Troy N. Smith Sr., opened its first location in 1953, under the name Top Hat Drive-In. Originally a walk-up root beer stand outside a log-cabin steakhouse selling soda, hamburgers, and hot dogs, Sonic expanded to over 3,400 locations in the United States. Sonic is known for its use of carhops on roller skates, and hosts an annual competition i

sampleRecalls.title: Autobrush Recalls Sonic Pro Children's Toothbrush Boxes Due to Risk of Serious Injury or Death from Battery Ingestion; Violates Mandatory Standard for Consumer Products with Coin Batteries
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Autobrush's Sonic Pro Kids toothbrush boxes used as packaging for delivering children's toothbrushes. The electric toothbrushes consist of a u-shaped mouthpiece and a plastic base with an animal's face that matches the model's name: Unity the Unicorn, Lenni the Lion, Harley the Hippo and Danny the…
hazards: The recalled delivery boxes violate the mandatory standard for consumer products containing button cell and coin batteries because they contain a lithium coin …
products: Autobrush Sonic Pro Kids Toothbrush Boxes

sampleRecalls.title: Fujitsu Recalls Battery Packs for Fujitsu Notebook Computers and Workstations Due to Fire and Burn Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Panasonic lithium-ion battery packs for these Fujitsu notebook computers and workstations: CELSIUS H720, LIFEBOOK E752, E733, E743, E753, P702, P772, S710, S752, S762, T732, T734, and T902. Recalled battery pack product numbers are CP556150-03, CP579060-01 and CP629458-03. The product and serial n…
hazards: The lithium-ion battery packs can overheat, posing burn and fire hazards to consumers.
injuries: Fujitsu has received one report of a battery fire in Canada.
products: Battery packs used in Fujitsu notebook computers and workstations.
units: About 5,800 in the U.S. and 606 in Canada

sampleRecalls.title: Panasonic Recalls Flat Screen Televisions and Swivel Stands Due to Tip-Over Hazard
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Panasonic 55-inch, LED/LCD flat screen televisions with a tabletop swivel stand intended for use in hotels, government buildings and schools. The televisions are black and "Panasonic" is printed on the front. The television's model number is TH055LRU50. The model number is printed on a tag located…
hazards: The mounting screws that connect the swivel stand to the television can come loose and cause the television to tip over and fall off the stand unexpectedly, po…
products: Panasonic 55-inch flat screen LED/LCD televisions with tabletop swivel stands
units: About 755 in the U.S. (In addition, 130 were sold in Canada)

sampleRecalls.title: Sonic Smart Wheels Self-Balancing Scooters/Hoverboards Recalled by Dollar Mania Due to Explosion and Fire Hazards
sampleRecalls.description: This recall involves Sonic Smart Wheels self-balancing scooters, commonly referred to as hoverboards, with model number S-01 or SBW666SL printed on the bottom of the unit. The hoverboards have two wheels at either end of a platform and are powered by lithium-ion batteries. The hoverboards were sold in black, blue, gre…
hazards: The lithium-ion battery packs in the self-balancing scooters/hoverboards can overheat, posing a risk of the products smoking, catching fire and/or exploding.
injuries: The firm has received one report of a self-balancing scooter/hoverboard catching fire in Louisiana in 2017 resulting in approximately $40,000 of property damage to a consumer's home. No injuries have…

sampleRecalls.title: Sony Expands Recall of VAIO Laptop Computer Battery Packs Due to Burn and Fire Hazards (Recall Alert)
sampleRecalls.description: This expanded recall involves Panasonic lithium-ion battery packs installed in 18 models of Sony's VAIO Series laptop computers. Sony has expanded the number of affected battery packs under this recall. The Panasonic battery packs were manufactured with the laptop and battery packs were sold separately or installed by…
hazards: The lithium-ion battery packs can overheat, posing burn and fire hazards.
products: Panasonic battery packs used in Sony Electronics laptop computers
units: About 700 (In addition, about 1,700 units were previously recalled on June 15, 2016)

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slip or trip
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was running to assist a nurse with a combative patient. As the employee turned a corner, he slipped and fell. The employee was hospitalized with a knee injury.

sampleRecords.nature: Cuts, lacerations, punctures without injury to internal structures
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was assisting animal control personnel with loading a stray dog into their truck when the dog bit the employee's face. The employee was hospitalized with facial injuries that required surgery.

sampleRecords.nature: Closed trauma involving internal organs, major blood vessels
sampleRecords.event_description: Pedestrian struck by vehicle backing up in nonroadway area
sampleRecords.final_narrative: Two employees were struck by an automobile that was backing up; one of the employees was hospitalized with a fractured skull and internal bleeding.

sampleRecords.event_description: Ignition of vapors, gases, or liquids
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was using a torque pump to tighten a frac valve when a spark ignited flammable gas and caused a flash fire. The employee sustained burns to their neck and arms.

sampleRecords.event_description: Fall on same level due to slipping
sampleRecords.final_narrative: An employee was performing opening duties at a car wash when he slipped on ice and fell on the sidewalk. He sustained a right leg fracture.
